county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1868 as one of the five original counties when Wyoming Territory was organized. Carbon County carved from Albany in 1868.
county_metadata.research_tips: Albany County Clerk in Laramie holds deed records from 1868. Home to the University of Wyoming (founded 1886), whose American Heritage Center holds extensive ranching, railroad, and territorial papers. The Laramie Plains Museum has local genealogical files. Laramie was a Union Pacific railroad town — check UP employee records at the Omaha archives. Territorial-era homestead records are at NARA Denver. Women's suffrage began here in 1869; early voter rolls may list women. Albany Heritage book covers pioneer families in detail.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1868 to present. County Clerk maintains index books.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1868. County Clerk issued licenses; state registration from 1941.

Federal population schedules for Albany County. Wyoming Territory enumerated from 1870. Agricultural and industrial schedules provide additional family details.
Historical newspapers from Albany County communities. Wyoming Newspaper Project digitized selected titles. Chronicling America has Wyoming papers from 1849.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery inscriptions, burial records, and sexton records from Albany County cemeteries. Many transcribed by DAR and local historical societies.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1897 from parts of Johnson, Sheridan, and Fremont counties. Washakie County later carved from Big Horn in 1911.
county_metadata.research_tips: Big Horn County Clerk in Basin has deed and vital records from 1897. Big Horn County Museum and Historical Society hold pioneer family files. The Burlington Route railroad (CB&Q) brought settlers — check railroad land records. Irrigation projects along the Big Horn River shaped settlement; Bureau of Reclamation records at NARA Denver document water rights and homesteads. Check Sheridan and Johnson counties for pre-1897 family records. German-Russian immigrant families settled the irrigated farmlands.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1911 from parts of Crook and Weston counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Campbell County Clerk in Gillette maintains records from 1911. The Rockpile Museum houses local history collections including ranching artifacts and family records. Campbell County was sparsely settled until the coal boom of the 1970s; early records center on cattle ranching families. Check Crook and Weston counties for pre-1911 records. The Wyoming State Archives in Cheyenne hold territorial-era stock brand registrations useful for identifying early ranching families in this area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1868 as one of the five original counties of Wyoming Territory. Named for coal deposits.
county_metadata.research_tips: Carbon County Clerk in Rawlins has records from 1868. Union Pacific Railroad was the dominant force — Rawlins, Hanna, and Carbon were railroad and coal towns. The 1903 Hanna mine disaster killed 169 miners; many were Finnish and Italian immigrants identifiable through mine company records and coroner's inquests. Carbon County Museum in Rawlins has local genealogical files. Chinese railroad workers are documented in UP records. Check NARA Denver for homestead entries along the UP corridor. Overland Trail crossed the county; emigrant diaries at the WY State Archives mention Carbon County landmarks.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1888 from parts of Laramie and Albany counties. Niobrara County later carved out in 1911.
county_metadata.research_tips: Converse County Clerk in Douglas has records from 1888. The Wyoming Pioneer Memorial Museum in Douglas is one of the state's best local history collections and holds family files, photographs, and homestead records. Fort Fetterman (est. 1867) military post records at NARA. Douglas was a key cattle shipping point on the Fremont, Elkhorn & Missouri Valley Railroad — check railroad land sales. The Texas Trail cattle drives passed through; stock growers' association records document brands and ranchers. Annual State Fair records may name local families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1875 from Laramie County. Campbell and Weston counties later carved from Crook.
county_metadata.research_tips: Crook County Clerk in Sundance holds records from 1875. Sundance was the county seat from formation — the Sundance Kid took his alias from the town jail. The Black Hills gold rush (1875-76) brought prospectors through Crook County. Crook County Museum and 1875 Art Gallery have local history materials. Early settlement was ranching-focused; territorial brand books at WY State Archives identify stockmen. Check Laramie County for pre-1875 records. Devils Tower area homesteads documented in GLO records at BLM.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1884 from Sweetwater County. Largest county in Wyoming by area. Contains Wind River Reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Fremont County Clerk in Lander has records from 1884. Wind River Reservation (Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho) dominates the county's history — tribal records at the BIA Wind River Agency and NARA Denver are essential for Native American genealogy. Fort Washakie military post records at NARA document soldiers and agency interactions. The South Pass gold mining district (1860s-70s) produced early settlement records. Dubois and Riverton have local museums with family files. Lander Pioneer Museum has extensive photographic and family history collections. Check Sweetwater County for pre-1884 territorial records.
